assessor_raw:
## Baseline Assessment (Run 1): Retrieval-Augmented Transformer for Enterprise Support

**Strengths:**
- Correctly implemented temporal split (months 0–24 / 25–30 / 31–36)
- Near-duplicate removal (>95% overlap)
- Stratified by product version and issue frequency quintile
- Incomplete labeling handled consistently

**Primary concern — metric mismatch:**
Macro AUROC is the primary metric and model selection criterion. Enterprise support agents see the top 3–5 KB article suggestions. Macro AUROC measures global score separation at all rank positions — a model can rank relevant articles at position 8 vs. position 30 and achieve a large AUROC gain that produces no operational difference for agents. The secondary metrics (Recall@10, NDCG@1, MRR) directly capture top-k quality, but model selection uses AUROC. The winning model on AUROC may not be the winning model on Recall@5.

**Secondary concern — baseline asymmetry:**
The baseline has pre-declared fixed hyperparameters and receives no validation-set tuning. The retrieval model undergoes broad hyperparameter search. The design frames this as keeping the baseline as a "stable lexical reference" — but this is methodologically equivalent to designing the baseline to lose. Any measured advantage for the transformer cannot be attributed to architectural superiority alone when tuning effort is not equalized.

**Verdict: critique_wins** — Both metric and comparison asymmetry issues are material.
